title: Qualys VMDR - Reporting Model For Snapshot And Period Metrics
applies_to: Engine-built vulnerability reports for assets, asset groups, and tags
Snapshot, previous month, previous quarter, and trend reporting from Qualys VMDR
canonical_reporting_grain: One vulnerability detection instance on one asset, enriched by KnowledgeBase metadata and asset inventory context.

report_modes.timestamp_basis: Extraction timestamp plus current detection status.
usage: Current open vulnerabilities for a single asset
Current severity distribution for a tag
Current top vulnerability titles across a selected asset scope

report_modes.description: Activity inside a defined date window such as previous month or previous quarter.
report_modes.timestamp_basis: One explicit event timestamp chosen for the question: FIRST_FOUND_DATETIME, LAST_FOUND_DATETIME, LAST_FIXED_DATETIME, LAST_UPDATE_DATETIME, or LAST_PROCESSED_DATETIME.
usage: Detections updated during the requested reporting period

report_modes.description: Repeated snapshot or repeated period activity grouped into month or quarter buckets.
report_modes.timestamp_basis: Consistent bucket rule selected by the report definition.
usage: Month-over-month fixed versus newly found comparison

metric_catalog: aging from first found to extraction or fix date

confirmed_facts.fact: Qualys documents QDS as a 1 to 100 score with four severity bands: Critical 90-100, High 70-89, Medium 40-69, and Low 1-39.
The Vulnerability Details page presents QID, Title, QDS, and Severity as primary reporting columns in the VMDR UI.

Qualys severity levels are assigned in the KnowledgeBase and are based on security risk, exploitation complexity, vendor-provided severity, prevalence, and known attacks, and managers can edit the severity for KB vulnerabilities.
The Host Detection List API exposes the timestamps needed to derive period-based reporting, including first found, last found, last update, and last processed dates, with optional reopened details.

implementation_notes: Make the timestamp basis explicit in every period report. For example, 'previous month by first found date' is materially different from 'previous month by last updated date'.
For current open-vulnerability reports, use current statuses from the detection feed and exclude fully fixed rows unless the user explicitly asks for resolved items.
For point-in-time reports by tag or asset group, resolve the asset scope first, then pull detections for that scope, then enrich with KB titles and CVEs.

For monthly or quarterly trends, persist normalized extraction results or reproducible snapshots. The reviewed docs provide enough timestamps to build trends, but not a historical dashboard API that reconstructs old UI states for you.
Keep both detection severity and KB severity available. If the report asks only for severity, default to the customer-visible Qualys severity dimension and note when QDS is also available.
caveats: The reviewed sources support building report logic, but they do not fully document how every VMDR dashboard widget is internally rolled up.
A true historical point-in-time snapshot for an old date usually requires stored historical extracts or snapshot persistence outside the live APIs.
Period-based reports can be misleading if the timestamp basis is implicit. The engine should spell out the chosen basis in report output.
